title: scabiosa crenata subsp. pseudisetensis
intro: Scabiosa crenata subsp. pseudisetensis, also known as the piney scabiosa, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Scabiosa crenata subsp. pseudisetensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Scabiosa crenata subsp. pseudisetensis can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
